commands.review_summary:
The PR raises Codex app-server `message` dynamic tool calls to a 120s default timeout while leaving ordinary dynamic tools at 30s and adding focused resolver coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main gives `message` dynamic tool calls the generic 30s Codex watchdog while Telegram text sends can use 60s per request and multiple sequential chunks. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer and CI handling for an already-focused PR.

target.title: fix(codex): extend message tool timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- give the Codex dynamic message tool a 120s default timeout
- keep the existing 30s default for ordinary dynamic tools
- add regression coverage for message-tool timeout resolution

## Why
Telegram text sends can take longer than 30s because outbound text requests have a 60s channel timeout and long messages are chunked/sent sequentially. The Codex dynamic-tool watchdog was timing out first, masking the underlying channel result and causing long scheduled digests to fail explicit message tool sends.

## Tests
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "message dynamic tool"

## Notes
- Full run-attempt.test.ts currently has two unrelated failures around extra thread/unsubscribe requests in image-cleanup binding tests; the new focused regression passes.
